today_news.title: OpenAI launches Claude Mythos rival GPT-5.6 Sol under government access it calls unsustainable
today_news.summary: OpenAI's new flagship GPT-5.6 Sol beats Anthropic's Claude Mythos 5 in coding benchmarks, but the US government is forcing a restricted rollout. OpenAI isn't happy about it.

today_news.title: AI startup Lindy ditched Claude entirely for Deepseek, saving millions as cost pressure mounts on Anthropic
today_news.summary: AI startup Lindy ditched Claude entirely for Deepseek after AI costs exceeded personnel costs. CEO Flo Crivello calls it "a matter of survival for the business."

today_news.title: ByteDance's "iLLaDA" is a diffusion language model that keeps up with Qwen2.5
today_news.summary: Researchers from Renmin University and ByteDance have released iLLaDA, an 8B language model that generates text differently than ChatGPT. It matches Qwen2.5 at the base level but falls behind after fine-tuning.

today_news.title: The Download: brain-melting heatwaves and unprecedented OpenAI restrictions

today_news.title: Altman won't go public for less than $1 trillion, so OpenAI's IPO may slip to 2027
today_news.summary: Advisors are telling OpenAI to hold off on going public until next year. The triggers: volatile tech markets and SpaceX's weak stock performance after its record IPO. SoftBank, one of OpenAI's biggest backers, lost 13 percent in a single day.

today_news.title: An AI model programmed nonstop for 19 days on a single MirrorCode task that cost $2,600 to run
today_news.summary: Epoch AI's new MirrorCode benchmark tests whether AI models can recreate complete programs without access to the original code. Claude Opus 4.7 leads with a 56 percent solve rate, rebuilding a 16,000-line toolkit in just 14 hours. But every model tested still fails on the most complex tasks.

today_news.title: Heat waves mess with your brain. Scientists are trying to figure out why.
today_news.summary: It’s been hot in London this week. Really hot. A dangerous heat wave has hit Western Europe. Yesterday, the UK recorded its highest ever June temperature at 36.1 °C (about 97 °F). today_tech.title: Refusal Lives Downstream of Persona in Chat Models
today_tech.summary: Abstract: Linear directions in activation space have been identified for both refusal and persona traits in instruction-tuned chat models, but the two have been studied as separate mechanisms.
